exemplar.title: In what ways has Elon Muskâs acquisition of X (Twitter) impacted its end users?

detailedExemplarData.title: In what ways has Elon Muskâs acquisition of X (Twitter) impacted its end users?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear identification and consistent reference to âchangeâ as the key concept
Strong links made between changes at X Corp and impacts on brand equity, revenue, and user experience
weaknesses: Analysis often sign-posted rather than fully integrated at sentence level
Occasional generic statements on AI and social-media trends dilute focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student demonstrates a good understanding of the key concept and clearly applies it to X Corp, the concept is sign-posted rather than woven seamlessly. This aligns with band 3 (âgood understanding with clear analytical thinkingâ) rather than the higher band requiring fully integrated analysis.

strengths: Appropriate number of supporting documents (four) meeting the 3â5 requirement
All documents cited are clearly relevant to content moderation, subscription data, or revenue trends
weaknesses: Depth is unevenâseveral sources quoted only once and analysis relies on secondary summaries
Limited range of primary evidence reduces breadth of ideas
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The four documents chosen are relevant and meet quantity requirements, but lack of depth in analysis and over-reliance on secondary sources limit this to band 2 (âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ).

strengths: Use of STEEPLE and a powerâinterest grid demonstrates basic application of tools
Descriptive statistics are directly applied to the research question
weaknesses: Selection of models is limitedâno financial ratios or strategic matrices
Application often remains descriptive with limited strategic implication
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the tools chosen are appropriate, the narrow selection and mostly descriptive application place this at band 2 (âsome tools and theories selected, superficial relevance to research questionâ).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of quantitative figures (brand awareness, revenue drops) to support claims
Attempts to link Muskâs strategic aims to stakeholder impacts
weaknesses: Analysis lacks sustained synthesisâSTEEPLE and data summaries often restate rather than evaluate
No triangulation over time or comparison to baselines weakens depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates some analysis but it is intermittent and largely descriptive, fitting band 2 (âsuperficial selection and use of data; limited analysis of research questionâ).

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, summarizing positive and negative impacts
Acknowledges study limitations and suggests areas for further investigation
weaknesses: Conclusions do not restate key statistics to justify claims
Some conclusions are not fully consistent with all evidence presented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the research question is clearly addressed, the linkage to the full range of evidence is incomplete, aligning with band 2 (âsome conclusions consistent with evidence; partial answerâ).

strengths: Clear table of contents and logical flow through introduction, analysis sections, and conclusion
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in heading levels and occasional repetition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is well organized with an appropriate structure throughout, meriting band 2.

strengths: All required elements (title, research question, candidate code, word count, references, appendices) are present
Appendix and bibliography are complete and correctly formatted
weaknesses: Inconsistent footnote and citation formatting interrupts readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With every required element included, the presentation meets band 2 criteria despite minor formatting issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is present (introduction, analysis sections, conclusion, bibliography, appendices) and is navigable through a contents table, satisfying the âappropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor; minor inconsistencies in heading levels and occasional repetition hold it back from perfection.

The chosen documents are clearly relevant (e.g., X Global Transparency Report for content-moderation data; Similarweb statistics for subscription uptake) and each provides usable quantitative or qualitative detail.  Depth is satisfactory but not outstanding: several documents are quoted only once and analysis occasionally relies on secondary news summaries rather than the primary evidence itself, so the strand aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth and wide range of ideasâ.

Title, research question, candidate code, word count, acknowledgements, references, appendices and required analytical tools are all present.  No required element appears missing, so the IA satisfies the highest presentation band.

Four supporting documents (transparency report, Musk statements, subscription statistics and policy excerpt) are cited and labelled in the text, comfortably meeting the 3-5 requirement.  The total number is therefore appropriate for the mid-upper bands but, with only four, does not demonstrate the breadth sometimes seen in the very top band.

Each tool is applied directly to Twitter/X: STEEPLE factors are populated with current data, and the grid positions stakeholders logically.  However, analysis sometimes stops at description (e.g., listing STEEPLE points without exploring strategic implications) so relevance is clear but application effectiveness is mixed.  This aligns with âadequately applied; relevance not always clearâ rather than the fully effective top band.

The student selects a reasonable range of quantitative and qualitative dataâadvertiser spend figures, subscription numbers, hate-speech statistics, brand-awareness percentagesâand uses them within the analysis.  Selection is **adequate** but not consistently triangulated or compared over time, preventing classification as âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ.

Analysis moves beyond narrationâlinking Muskâs strategic aims to user impacts and to stakeholder reactionsâyet gaps remain.  For instance, the STEEPLE table is followed by commentary that reiterates points rather than synthesising them, and evaluation rarely contrasts alternative viewpoints.  Consequently the work demonstrates âsome analysisâ with intermittent effectiveness, matching the band 3 descriptor.

The student explicitly answers the research questionâstating overall negative impacts on end users and summarising positive versus negative effectsâmeeting the descriptor for the top band in this strand.

The IA employs STEEPLE, a power-interest grid and descriptive statistics.  This represents an **adequate, textbook selection** of tools appropriate to the research question, yet lacks variety in analytical depth (no financial ratios, Boston Matrix, Ansoff, etc.).  Hence it satisfies âadequate selectionâ rather than âeffective selectionâ.

The conclusion references earlier evidence (brand-awareness decline, hate-speech concerns, subscription uptake) but omits others, and supporting statistics are not restated to justify final claims.  Thus **some** conclusions are consistent with presented data, yet linkage is not comprehensive, fitting the mid-level band.

Throughout the IA the student links evidence, tools and commentary directly to X Corp specifics: brand-awareness data, advertiser revenue, subscription uptake, transparency reports and survey results.  The STEEPLE and power-interest grid are explicitly populated with figures and quotations about X, not generic statements.  This provides **strong, organisation-specific analysis** and clear integration most of the time; minor lapses (e.g., brief generic statements on AI or global social-media trends) prevent the exhaustive level expected for full marks, so the âstrong but not fully integratedâ band is most suitable.

The student correctly selects âchangeâ as the key concept and repeatedly anchors discussion of the acquisition, re-branding, content-moderation shifts and introduction of X Premium to specific *changes* made by Musk.  These references move beyond simple mention and are linked to impacts on brand equity, revenue streams and user experience, demonstrating **strong conceptual understanding**.  Integration is visible in every major section, yet the concept tends to be sign-posted (âthis changeâ¦â) rather than woven seamlessly into all analytical sentences; therefore the work fits the descriptor of âpartially integrated analysisâ rather than the fully holistic integration required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The student clearly identifies and consistently references âChangeâ as the key concept, anchoring the investigationâs framework. This establishes a focused conceptual lens.
All required presentation elements (title, concept, candidate code, word count) are present and correctly formatted, satisfying presentation requirements.
The table of contents is clear and logically structured, providing navigability and demonstrating an appropriate organizational flow.
The concept of change is repeatedly sign-posted rather than woven seamlessly into analysis. Consider integrating âchangeâ within each analytical sentence to deepen conceptual integration.
Several key points rely on secondary news summaries rather than primary evidence. Use more primary documents (e.g., company releases) to enhance document relevance and depth.
The STEEPLE tableâs environmental cell is truncated, lacking specifics. Expand with concrete metrics (e.g., carbon footprint, data center energy use) to deepen analysis.
Rebranding analysis repeatedly uses âchangeâ in bold. Instead, weave the concept into explanations (e.g., âthe rebrand exemplifies transformative change byâ¦â).
While STEEPLE, powerâinterest grid and descriptive statistics are appropriate, the inclusion of additional models (e.g., Ansoff Matrix or financial ratios) would strengthen theoretical breadth.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Footnote citations display inconsistencies (missing author formatting, stray line breaks). Standardize reference style to improve presentation clarity.
In Table 2, column headers and content are misaligned, and the blank AI usage header row disrupts readability. Clarify headings and cell structure.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The STEEPLE narrative summaries outline factors but do not synthesise their strategic implications. Develop deeper evaluation by linking factors and assessing trade-offs.
The discussion cites a 90% brand awareness figure, effectively using industry data to support claims about user perceptions.
Link the rebrandâs effect on user behaviour (e.g., session length, engagement) to revenue impacts to deepen your evaluation.
The citation block includes stray LaTeX fractions, which interrupt readability. Remove or correct these artifacts in footnote formatting.
The bibliography covers a wide range of primary and secondary sources, demonstrating thorough supporting document selection.
The inclusion of appendix A and a full reference list ensures all required elements are present, meeting high presentation standards.
Supporting documents are limited to key reports; consider adding more diverse primary data (e.g., internal memos, user interviews) to increase depth and range.
The content moderation section presents percentages but stops short of evaluating end-user consequences. Discuss how these figures translate into user trust or platform safety.
Consider triangulating the YouGov survey results with platform usage or moderation data to strengthen the depth of supporting evidence.
The power-interest gridâs layout lacks labels for minimal contact and keep satisfied rows, reducing clarity. Ensure each cell is clearly demarcated.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Discussion of minimal contact stakeholders could explore strategic implications for Xâs relationship management rather than mere placement.
Linking the X Premium changes directly to user impacts and subscriber behavior demonstrates a strong connection to the research question.
Use of advertiser revenue drop figures showcases effective data selection and strengthens the analysis of stakeholder impacts.
Subscription uptake analysis cites sign-up and deactivation rates but lacks a baseline for comparison. Provide previous period metrics to contextualize these changes.
Acknowledging the lack of publicly available premium subscription data demonstrates critical awareness of data limitations.
The conclusionâs bullet lists clearly separate positive and negative changes, but individual points would benefit from specific evidence citations for consistency.
The identification of study limitations and suggestion for further factors (algorithms, culture) strengthens the conclusionâs critical evaluation.
